title: mutisia cochabambensis
intro: Mutisia cochabambensis, also known as the Cochabamban Mutisia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have health benefits such as reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Mutisia cochabambensis are typically made from the plant's leaves and roots, which can be consumed as teas, infusions, or applied topically as creams or ointments.
